summary:
Claim: Approximately 15–75 muscle fibers typically attach to a single Golgi tendon organ.  
Evidence: - Modern neurophysiological studies show broad variation in fiber-to-GTO connectivity, especially in large or high-load muscles. - Functional redundancy and biomechanical demands justify higher-than-expected fiber counts per GTO.  
Answer: D
